title: Will Rebuild of Evangelion get affected by Bill 156
display_title: Will Rebuild of Evangelion get affected by Bill 156
wikitext:
[]: Will Rebuild of Evangelion get affected by Bill 156 ?             Quote Post by bladerj » Wed Dec 15, 2010 11:48 am Tokyo’s manga ban has been signed into law after passing a final vote, and is set to come into effect in 2011, with Tokyo governor Ishihara crowing that “Japan still has some common sense left after all!” The ban already passed a committee vote, and as expected also passed a final vote on the 15th – the law stipulates that “voluntary restraints” must begun in April of 2011, with the full force of the ban to come in July. All the major parties supported the ban – the ruling DPJ (leftist) insisted on a “prudent application” rider but otherwise did nothing to oppose the law as they did with the previous bill. The LDP (right-wing) proposed and supported the original and current version of the bill. Their usual coalition partners, the Koumeito (the political arm of a Buddhist cult), also supported the bill, leaving only small parties to the left of the DPJ to oppose it. Once again the motives of the DPJ can only be guessed at – they have repeatedly blocked national bans on possession of child pornography (already illegal to distribute) on civil liberties grounds, opposed the previously rejected version of the bill because it was worded as a ban on “virtual child pornography,” and actually insisted the just passed Tokyo law remove a “duty not to possess [real] child pornography” clause. However, when the current bill’s wording was changed to ban depictions “promoting illegal or immoral sexual activity” (effectively a vastly more far-reaching ban than previously) rather than “[virtual] sexual activity involving minors” (this version of the ban was clearly targeting loli manga) as in the current version, their previous opposition all but evaporated. That the law explicitly excludes photographic material can probably be interpreted both as a specific attack on “otaku” culture and an effort to avoid antagonising Japan’s mass media, who obviously would not support any restrictions on themselves but are only too happy to support them on other industries. The 10 publishers who have boycotted the Tokyo Anime Fair are angry at the treatment meted out to the industry by Ishihara and his cronies: “The earlier bill was defeated with heavy opposition, and we are indignant that the bill should be resubmitted in so short a time.”

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 15, 2010 11:53 am Edited title to be more accurate -- the measure allows you to sell your Adult-Only loli doujins (and pre-teen idol DVDs) exactly as before.

[]: Re: Will Rebuild of Evangelion get affected by Bill 156 ?             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Wed Dec 15, 2010 12:04 pm bladerj wrote: I don't think (hope) the plug suits will be affected, but fanservice on movies and stuff like the test plug and all those Rei*/Asuka moe illustrations will problably bannable in any media !!! I expect kharas income to drop 99,9990236% !!! What of the future of the movies !!!

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Dec 15, 2010 12:08 pm Evangelion is too much of a cultural institution by this point aka it's going to get "lolartistic merit" saving grace

[]:             Quote Post by CORE » Wed Dec 15, 2010 12:47 pm Xard wrote: Evangelion is too much of a cultural institution by this point aka it's going to get "lolartistic merit" saving grace

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Dec 15, 2010 1:44 pm I don't understand shit you're saying. Technically they could "outlaw" Evangelion if they wanted. If EoE were to come out next year it would end up with 18+ tag in a heartbeat. But eva's institution, no newbie show with suspicious and controversial reputation. Hence it's safe.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Wed Dec 15, 2010 6:32 pm Yeah, let's get worried. Sound the alarm bells.

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Wed Dec 15, 2010 6:33 pm You have to remember that these films are being presented in theaters, so if anything, there might be a slight adjustment in the rating of these films. (If the Rebuild's were PG 13, under this new law they might be 14A)

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Wed Dec 15, 2010 7:20 pm I'm not talking about just the movie, think of those illustrations sadamoto does to promote the movies in diferent merchandising sexualizing the girls, that are 14 ,and thus minors.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Dec 15, 2010 7:25 pm I don't think Evangelion could be seen as unjustifiably glorifying/exaggerating sexual or pseudo-sexual activity. Unless Q seems to give the message that Shinji having sex is the right thing to do, it'll be fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Wed Dec 15, 2010 7:25 pm bladerj wrote: I'm not talking about just the movie, think of those illustrations sadamoto does to promote the movies in diferent merchandising sexualizing the girls, that are 14 ,and thus minors.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Dec 15, 2010 7:28 pm Age of consent and age of majority are not necessarily the same thing even in the United States.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Wed Dec 15, 2010 11:54 pm It makes sense for this to have passed – Japan still has some common sense left after all!

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Thu Dec 16, 2010 12:18 am Mr. Tines wrote: Edited title to be more accurate -- the measure allows you to sell your Adult-Only loli doujins (and pre-teen idol DVDs) exactly as before.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Dec 16, 2010 2:30 am @skikes My understanding, based on sifting a lot of froth on /a/ (so to be taken with a large pinch of salt) is - they use a very coarse grained rating system (everyone or adult-only) for most things - adult-only means porn of some sort - stores sell one or the other (you have to go out of your way to get the adult-only material; in particular you won't find it on the top shelf of the rack with your Shonen Jump -- unless the extreme doomsayers are right and that gets AO rated too) - AO material wouldn't be broadcast except perhaps upon some subscription channels (think the uncensored versions of anime appearing on the AT-X channel) - the bill explicitly covers anime and manga only (so junior idol videos are still open access) - the bill also contains some requirement to put age-related controls on mobile phone internet access I have no idea what if any sort of system of rating for movies is used, nor how things are sorted into the different buckets. However, I expect -- as Xard suggests -- that cultural icons like Evangelion (and One Piece, Doraemon and Sazae-san) will get a bye on grounds of artistic merit. See also http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=407505#407505 The main impact on Rebuild looks like it would depend on whether Shinji/Rei is a) considered a relationship that could not end in marriage, b) they get down to business on screen, c) it's deemed to be excessive glorification of such.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Thu Dec 16, 2010 3:46 am Mr. Tines wrote: @skikes My understanding, based on sifting a lot of froth on /a/ (so to be taken with a large pinch of salt) is...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Dec 16, 2010 3:48 am skikes wrote: I see... wouldn't putting an age rating on it and requiring ID for purchase work just as well?

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Thu Dec 16, 2010 5:56 pm No matter what kind of control these guys do, some can just simply override it, like here in the Philippines for example. Kids get to view AO materials by lying about their age. if you're familiar with sites that ask for your age in order to enter, then you'll get what I mean.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Thu Dec 16, 2010 7:32 pm You can do THAT ? *makes inocent face

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Thu Dec 16, 2010 10:04 pm I seriously can't believe anyone will think Evangelion will be rated as "adult only." This stuff was originally intended for kids after all. But if it will be, it will be.
